questions.learningObjective: Identify the flight parameters derived from a pitot-static smart probe.
questions.fullObjective: Given a pitot-static smart probe installation, the technician shall identify airspeed, altitude, and vertical speed as the flight parameters supplied to the air data system.
questions.targetSourceStem: What flight parameters does a pitot-static smart probe measure for the air data system?

flashcard.definition: Airspeed, altitude, and vertical speed, derived from the probe's total and static pressures.

jeopardy.clue: A pitot-static smart probe delivers these three core flight parameters to the air data system.
choices: Autopilot command signals only
Outside air temperature only
Airspeed, altitude, vertical speed
Engine RPM and torque values
jeopardy.explanation: Air data produces airspeed, altitude, and vertical speed from total and static pressures.

multipleChoice.stem: A pitot-static smart probe provides the air data system with which set of parameters?
choices: Engine RPM and shaft torque
Outside air temperature by itself
Airspeed, altitude, and vertical speed
Autopilot command discretes

practice.stem: An instructor asks which parameters the air data system derives from a pitot-static smart probe. Which answer should the technician provide?
choices: Outside air temperature by itself
Engine RPM and shaft torque values
Airspeed, altitude, and vertical speed
Autopilot command outputs to the servos

preassessment.stem: Which flight parameters does a pitot-static smart probe supply to the air data system?
choices: Engine RPM and torque values
Outside air temperature only
Airspeed, altitude, vertical speed
Autopilot command outputs only
preassessment.explanation: From total and static pressures the air data system derives airspeed, altitude, and vertical speed; OAT and engine parameters come from separate sensors.

questions.learningObjective: Describe what makes a pitot-static probe a smart probe.
questions.fullObjective: Given a comparison of traditional and smart pitot-static probes, the technician shall describe the defining feature as an integrated digital processor that converts pressures into digital data.
questions.targetSourceStem: What makes a pitot-static probe a 'smart' probe compared to traditional pneumatic probes?

flashcard.term: Integrated digital processor producing air data output
flashcard.definition: An integrated digital processor that converts pressure into digital air data onboard the probe.

jeopardy.clue: This defining feature sets a 'smart' pitot-static probe apart from a conventional pneumatic one.
choices: A self-adjusting heater element only
A ceramic probe body material
A manual calibration requirement
A built-in digital processor

multipleChoice.stem: Compared to a traditional pneumatic probe, a pitot-static smart probe is distinguished by its:
choices: Ceramic construction in place of metal alloy
Only having a self-adjusting heater element
Need for routine manual calibration of pressures
Integrated digital processor producing air data output

practice.stem: A new technician asks why the term 'smart' is applied to modern pitot-static probes. Which feature makes them qualify?
choices: Each probe simply includes a self-adjusting anti-ice heater and nothing else.
Each probe is constructed from a ceramic material for weight savings.
Each probe requires recurring manual pressure calibration by maintenance.
Each probe contains an integrated digital processor producing air data words.

preassessment.stem: What makes a pitot-static probe a smart probe rather than a traditional pneumatic one?
choices: It has only a self-adjusting heater element.
It is constructed from ceramic material.
It requires manual calibration routines.
It contains an integrated digital processor.

questions.learningObjective: Explain the installation advantage of smart probes over traditional plumbing.
questions.fullObjective: Given a comparison between smart probes and traditional pitot-static plumbing, the technician shall explain that smart probes reduce tubing runs and overall system complexity.
questions.targetSourceStem: What advantage does a smart probe installation provide compared to traditional pitot-static plumbing?

flashcard.term: Reduced tubing runs and overall installation complexity
flashcard.definition: They sharply reduce pneumatic tubing runs and the mechanical complexity of the installation.

jeopardy.clue: This installation advantage is the main reason manufacturers are replacing traditional pitot-static plumbing with smart probes.
choices: Reduced tubing runs and complexity
Higher maintenance requirements
Simpler cockpit instrumentation
Lower overall system cost

multipleChoice.stem: Compared to a traditional pitot-static plumbing installation, a smart probe system provides:
choices: Reduced tubing runs and overall installation complexity
Higher scheduled maintenance task requirements
A simplified cockpit display instrumentation layout
A lower overall cost across the entire fleet life

practice.stem: A retrofit engineer is justifying replacing pneumatic pitot-static plumbing with smart probes. Which design advantage does he cite?
choices: The smart probe layout reduces pneumatic tubing runs and installation complexity.
The smart probe layout requires more frequent scheduled maintenance tasks.
The smart probe layout simplifies the cockpit display instrumentation itself.
The smart probe layout lowers the total lifecycle cost on every aircraft.

preassessment.stem: A smart probe installation offers which advantage over traditional pitot-static plumbing?
choices: Reduced tubing runs and installation complexity
Higher scheduled maintenance task load
Simpler cockpit display instrumentation
Lower lifecycle cost in every case

questions.learningObjective: Identify what pressure is measured at the static port of a pitot-static probe.
questions.fullObjective: Given a pitot-static probe, the technician shall identify that the static port measures the ambient atmospheric pressure surrounding the aircraft.
questions.targetSourceStem: What does the static pressure port on a pitot-static probe measure?

flashcard.term: Ambient atmospheric pressure around the aircraft
flashcard.definition: The ambient atmospheric pressure surrounding the aircraft, independent of ram air effects.

jeopardy.clue: This is the pressure that the static port on a pitot-static probe samples from the airstream.
choices: Cabin pressurization level
Ambient atmospheric pressure
Ram air dynamic pressure
Exhaust gas temperature

multipleChoice.stem: The static port on a pitot-static probe senses which pressure?
choices: Engine exhaust gas back pressure level
Ambient atmospheric pressure around the aircraft
Cabin pressurization differential pressure
Ram air dynamic pressure from forward motion

practice.stem: An apprentice asks why the static port is drilled perpendicular to the airflow instead of facing forward. Which pressure is this port supposed to measure?
choices: The pressurization differential inside the cabin
The ambient atmospheric pressure around the aircraft
The ram air dynamic pressure caused by forward flight
The back pressure in the engine exhaust stream

preassessment.stem: The static port on a pitot-static probe measures:
choices: Cabin pressurization level
Ambient atmospheric pressure
Ram air dynamic pressure
Exhaust gas back pressure

questions.learningObjective: Identify the dominant failure mode of pitot-static smart probes.
questions.fullObjective: Given historical smart probe failure data, the technician shall identify port blockage by ice or debris as the dominant failure mode causing pitot-static probe discrepancies.
questions.targetSourceStem: A technician is reviewing smart probe failure data. What failure mode causes the majority of pitot-static probe discrepancies?

flashcard.term: Blockage of pressure ports by ice or foreign debris
flashcard.definition: Blockage of the pressure ports by ice accretion or foreign debris.

jeopardy.clue: Field data shows this failure mode is the dominant cause of pitot-static smart probe discrepancies.
choices: Heater element open from thermal stress
Firmware corruption during power cycle
Blockage of ports by ice or debris
ARINC transmitter output stage failure

multipleChoice.stem: When reviewing smart probe fleet failure data, the technician sees that most discrepancies are caused by:
choices: Firmware corruption during a power cycle event
An open heater element from thermal stress cracking
Blockage of pressure ports by ice or foreign debris
Output stage failure of the ARINC transmitter chip

practice.stem: A reliability engineer is compiling smart probe fleet data for a review. Which failure mode does he report as the leading cause of pitot-static probe discrepancies?
choices: Opening of the heater element from repeated thermal stress
Corruption of the onboard firmware during a power cycle
Blockage of the pressure ports by ice or foreign debris
Output stage failure on the ARINC 429 transmitter chip

preassessment.stem: The majority of pitot-static smart probe discrepancies are caused by:
choices: Output stage failures on the ARINC transmitter
Firmware corruption during power cycling
Port blockage from ice or debris
Open heater elements from thermal stress

questions.learningObjective: Explain the purpose of the heating element inside a pitot probe.
questions.fullObjective: Given a pitot probe with a current-drawing heater, the technician shall explain that the heater's purpose is to prevent ice from blocking the probe's pressure ports.
questions.targetSourceStem: Pitot probes contain heating elements that draw significant electrical current. What is the purpose of these heaters?

flashcard.term: Prevent ice from blocking the pressure ports in flight
flashcard.definition: The heaters prevent ice from forming over and blocking the pressure ports in flight.

jeopardy.clue: The heating element inside a pitot probe draws heavy current for this specific reason.
choices: To stabilize the sensor temperature only
To warm air for temperature sensing
To supply power to the processor chip
To prevent ice from blocking the ports

multipleChoice.stem: The high-current heating element inside a pitot probe is installed in order to:
choices: Provide electrical power to the onboard processor
Stabilize the pressure sensor temperature only
Warm sampled air before temperature sensing occurs
Prevent ice from blocking the pressure ports in flight

practice.stem: A technician is explaining to an apprentice why pitot probes draw so much current. Which rationale should he give for the heater element?
choices: The heater warms the sampled air before the temperature sensor reads it.
The heater stabilizes the pressure sensor die temperature only.
The heater provides electrical power to the onboard probe processor.
The heater prevents ice from forming over and blocking the pressure ports.

preassessment.stem: The heating elements inside pitot probes are provided to:
choices: Power the onboard probe processor
Stabilize only the pressure sensor temperature
Warm sampled air for temperature sensing
Keep ice from blocking the pressure ports

questions.learningObjective: Identify the data protocol used by smart probes to send air data.
questions.fullObjective: Given a smart probe installation, the technician shall identify ARINC 429 digital data words as the typical transmission protocol for air data to the avionics.
questions.targetSourceStem: What data transmission protocol do smart probes typically use to send air data to the avionics?

flashcard.definition: ARINC 429 digital data words on a unidirectional twisted-shielded pair bus.

jeopardy.clue: This digital protocol is typically used by smart probes to transmit air data to the avionics suite.
choices: ARINC 429 digital data words
Analog voltage proportional to pressure
Radio frequency telemetry link
Mechanical cable position signal

multipleChoice.stem: Smart probes most commonly deliver air data to the avionics over which protocol?
choices: ARINC 429 digital data words
Analog DC voltage proportional to pressure
A radio frequency telemetry link
Mechanical cable position linkage signals

practice.stem: A shop technician is wiring a smart probe into a new air data interface. Which transmission protocol should he expect on the probe's output pair?
choices: ARINC 429 digital data words over a twisted shielded pair
A proportional analog DC voltage corresponding to the pressure
A UHF radio frequency telemetry link between probe and avionics
A mechanical position cable linking the probe to an indicator

preassessment.stem: Smart probes typically send air data to the avionics using which protocol?
choices: ARINC 429 digital data words
Analog voltage proportional to pressure
Radio frequency telemetry link
Mechanical cable position signal

questions.learningObjective: Identify the most common cause of inaccurate smart probe readings.
questions.fullObjective: Given inaccurate readings from a pitot-static smart probe, the technician shall identify blockage of the pressure sensing ports as the most common root cause.
questions.targetSourceStem: What is the most common cause of inaccurate readings from a pitot-static smart probe?

flashcard.term: Blockage of the pressure sensing ports
flashcard.definition: Physical blockage of the pressure sensing ports by ice, debris, or insects.

jeopardy.clue: Among all possible causes, this is the single most common source of inaccurate pitot-static smart probe readings.
choices: Interference from the weather radar
Blockage of the pressure sensing ports
A loss of GPS receiver signal lock
A malfunction in the air data computer

multipleChoice.stem: Inaccurate readings from a pitot-static smart probe are most commonly the result of:
choices: A loss of GPS receiver signal lock
Blockage of the pressure sensing ports
An internal air data computer malfunction
Radio frequency interference from weather radar

practice.stem: A pilot reports erratic airspeed and altitude readings after parking outside overnight. Which root cause should the technician investigate first on the smart probe system?
choices: An internal malfunction of the air data computer unit
Blockage of the pressure sensing ports on the probe body
A loss of GPS receiver satellite signal lock from above
Interference from the aircraft weather radar transmissions

preassessment.stem: Inaccurate pitot-static smart probe readings are most commonly caused by:
choices: Weather radar RF interference
Blockage of the pressure sensing ports
Loss of GPS receiver signal lock
An air data computer internal malfunction

questions.learningObjective: Identify the primary scheduled maintenance task for a pitot-static smart probe.
questions.fullObjective: Given a pitot-static smart probe between major inspections, the technician shall identify the primary scheduled maintenance task as a visual inspection of the ports for contamination.
questions.targetSourceStem: What is the primary scheduled maintenance task for pitot-static smart probes between major inspections?

flashcard.term: Visual inspection of the pressure ports for contamination
flashcard.definition: A visual inspection of the pressure ports for contamination, cracks, or obstruction.

jeopardy.clue: Between heavy inspections, this is the primary scheduled maintenance task performed on pitot-static smart probes.
choices: Quarterly heater element replacement
Monthly pressure sensor recalibration
Visual inspection of ports for contamination
Annual firmware update installation

multipleChoice.stem: Between major inspections, the primary scheduled maintenance for a pitot-static smart probe is:
choices: Quarterly replacement of the probe heater element
Monthly recalibration of the internal pressure sensor
Visual inspection of the pressure ports for contamination
Annual installation of a firmware update package

practice.stem: A line technician reviews the maintenance manual for a pitot-static smart probe. Which task is listed as the primary scheduled maintenance between major inspections?
choices: A quarterly replacement of the probe's heater element
A monthly recalibration of the internal pressure sensor
A visual inspection of the pressure ports for contamination
An annual installation of the latest firmware update

preassessment.stem: The primary scheduled maintenance task on a pitot-static smart probe between major inspections is:
choices: Monthly pressure sensor recalibration
Quarterly heater element replacement
Visual inspection of ports for contamination
Annual firmware update installation

questions.learningObjective: Identify the pressure measured by the pitot tube of a smart probe.
questions.fullObjective: Given a smart probe containing both pitot and static elements, the technician shall identify the pitot tube as measuring total pressure, which includes ram air contribution.
questions.targetSourceStem: A smart probe contains both pitot and static sensing elements. What specific pressure does the pitot tube measure?

flashcard.term: Total pressure, which includes ram air contribution
flashcard.definition: Total pressure, which is the sum of ambient static pressure and ram air dynamic pressure.

jeopardy.clue: In a pitot-static smart probe, this is the specific pressure the pitot tube element senses.
choices: Ambient static pressure only
Temperature-compensated pressure
Differential for vertical speed
Total pressure including ram air

multipleChoice.stem: Inside a smart probe, the pitot tube element measures:
choices: Only the ambient static atmospheric pressure
A temperature-compensated cabin reference pressure
A differential pressure used for vertical speed
Total pressure, which includes ram air contribution

practice.stem: A technician explains the internal breakdown of a smart probe. The pitot tube specifically samples what pressure?
choices: A differential pressure dedicated to computing the vertical speed
Ambient static atmospheric pressure from around the aircraft only
A temperature-compensated cabin pressurization reference signal
Total pressure, which is ambient pressure plus ram air dynamic pressure

preassessment.stem: The pitot tube element inside a smart probe senses:
choices: Temperature-compensated cabin pressure
Ambient static pressure only
Differential pressure for vertical speed
Total pressure including ram air

questions.learningObjective: Prioritize preflight inspection items for an inconsistent smart probe.
questions.fullObjective: Given inconsistent smart probe data during preflight checks, the technician shall prioritize inspecting the wiring connections and port condition first.
questions.targetSourceStem: A smart probe shows inconsistent data during preflight checks. What should the technician inspect first?

flashcard.term: Wiring connections and port condition on the probe
flashcard.definition: Wiring connections and the physical condition of the pressure ports on the probe body.

jeopardy.clue: When a smart probe shows inconsistent preflight data, these two items should be inspected first.
choices: The wiring connections and the port condition
The processor firmware version level
The pressure sensor calibration date
The power supply voltage regulation

multipleChoice.stem: A smart probe shows inconsistent data during preflight. The first item to inspect is:
choices: Wiring connections and port condition on the probe
The processor firmware version number installed
The pressure sensor calibration date sticker
The power supply voltage regulation output level

practice.stem: During a preflight test, the right-side smart probe repeatedly reports inconsistent air data values. Where should the line technician look first?
choices: At the wiring connections and the physical condition of the probe ports
At the installed processor firmware version number
At the calibration date recorded on the pressure sensor
At the regulation of the probe's DC power supply output

preassessment.stem: If a smart probe shows inconsistent preflight data, the first thing to inspect is:
choices: Wiring connections and port condition
Processor firmware version level
Pressure sensor calibration date
Power supply voltage regulation

questions.learningObjective: Describe the maintenance benefit provided by the smart probe microprocessor.
questions.fullObjective: Given a smart probe with an embedded microprocessor, the technician shall describe the maintenance benefit as continuous self-test and fault detection capability.
questions.targetSourceStem: What capability does the microprocessor in a smart probe provide for maintenance?

flashcard.term: Continuous self-test and fault detection output
flashcard.definition: Continuous self-test and fault detection that reports internal health to the air data system.

jeopardy.clue: The microprocessor inside a smart probe offers this key capability to support maintenance actions.
choices: Automatic probe weight reduction
Continuous self-test and fault detection
Improved mechanical durability
Multiple pressure type sensing

multipleChoice.stem: The onboard microprocessor in a smart probe supports maintenance by providing:
choices: An improvement in mechanical probe durability
Continuous self-test and fault detection output
An automatic reduction in probe weight
The ability to sense multiple pressure types

practice.stem: A shop foreman is explaining the maintenance advantage of a modern smart probe. Which capability, enabled by the built-in microprocessor, should he highlight?
choices: An improvement in mechanical durability under icing and bird strikes
Continuous self-test with fault detection reported to the air data system
An automatic weight reduction compared with conventional probe bodies
An ability to sense multiple pressure types using one sensing diaphragm

preassessment.stem: The microprocessor inside a smart probe provides what maintenance capability?
choices: Automatic probe weight reduction
Continuous self-test and fault detection
Improved mechanical durability margin
Multiple pressure type sensing modes

questions.learningObjective: Identify the primary safety consequence of a pitot-static smart probe failure.
questions.fullObjective: Given a pitot-static smart probe failure in flight, the technician shall identify erroneous airspeed and altitude data as the primary flight safety consequence.
questions.targetSourceStem: What is the primary flight safety consequence if a pitot-static smart probe fails during flight?

flashcard.definition: Erroneous airspeed and altitude data presented to the flight crew and autopilot.

jeopardy.clue: This is the primary flight safety consequence when a pitot-static smart probe fails in flight.
choices: Navigation display blanking only
Loss of radio communication ability
Erroneous airspeed and altitude data
Increased fuel consumption rate

multipleChoice.stem: The primary flight safety consequence of a pitot-static smart probe failure in flight is:
choices: Blanking of the navigation display only
Complete loss of radio communication ability
Erroneous airspeed and altitude data
An increase in cruise fuel consumption rate

practice.stem: A safety instructor is discussing consequences of a pitot-static smart probe failure in flight. Which consequence should he identify as the primary safety concern for the crew?
choices: An increase in the cruise fuel consumption rate of the engines
A complete loss of all radio communication capability on the aircraft
Erroneous airspeed and altitude data displayed to the crew and autopilot
Blanking of the multifunction navigation display without any other effect

preassessment.stem: A pitot-static smart probe failure in flight primarily causes:
choices: Blanking of the nav display only
Loss of all radio communication
Erroneous airspeed and altitude data
Higher cruise fuel consumption

questions.learningObjective: Identify the pressure inputs required by the air data computer.
questions.fullObjective: Given an air data computer, the technician shall identify pitot (total) pressure and static (ambient) pressure as the two pressure inputs required to compute flight parameters.
questions.targetSourceStem: What pressure inputs does the air data computer require to compute flight parameters?

flashcard.term: Pitot total and static ambient pressure
flashcard.definition: Pitot total pressure and static ambient pressure from the pitot-static system.

jeopardy.clue: These two pressure inputs are required by the air data computer to compute flight parameters.
choices: Engine manifold pressure only
GPS altitude and ground speed
Fuel tank quantity level signals
Pitot total and static ambient pressure

multipleChoice.stem: The air data computer requires which pressure inputs to compute flight parameters?
choices: Engine manifold pressure by itself
GPS altitude and GPS ground speed
Fuel tank quantity sensor inputs
Pitot total and static ambient pressure

practice.stem: A technician is wiring a new air data computer. Which two pressure inputs must be routed to it for it to compute airspeed and altitude?
choices: Only fuel tank quantity sensor level inputs
GPS altitude and GPS ground speed signals
Only engine manifold pressure from the turbine
Pitot total pressure and static ambient pressure

preassessment.stem: The air data computer computes flight parameters from which pressure inputs?
choices: Fuel tank quantity level signals
GPS altitude and GPS ground speed
Only engine manifold pressure input
Pitot total and static ambient pressure

questions.learningObjective: Identify the trigger condition for an unreliable airspeed warning on a multi-probe aircraft.
questions.fullObjective: Given a multi-probe aircraft, the technician shall identify disagreement between air data sources as the condition that triggers an unreliable airspeed warning.
questions.targetSourceStem: What condition triggers an 'unreliable airspeed' warning on a multi-probe aircraft?

flashcard.term: Disagreement between independent air data sources
flashcard.definition: Disagreement between independent air data sources beyond the system's comparison threshold.

jeopardy.clue: On a multi-probe aircraft, this condition trips the 'unreliable airspeed' warning to the crew.
choices: Disagreement between air data sources
Partial blockage on a single probe only
Loss of GPS satellite signal lock
A manual disconnect of the autopilot

multipleChoice.stem: On a multi-probe aircraft, an 'unreliable airspeed' warning is triggered by:
choices: Disagreement between independent air data sources
A partial blockage detected on a single probe only
Loss of satellite signal lock by the GPS receiver
A manual disconnect of the autopilot system

practice.stem: A new technician asks how a multi-probe aircraft decides to annunciate unreliable airspeed. Which triggering condition should be cited?
choices: A disagreement between the independent air data sources beyond a threshold
A partial blockage detected on only one of the installed probes
A loss of satellite signal lock by the GPS receiver in flight
A manual disconnection of the autopilot by the flight crew

preassessment.stem: On a multi-probe aircraft, the unreliable airspeed warning is triggered by:
choices: Disagreement between air data sources
A partial blockage on one probe only
Loss of GPS satellite signal lock
Manual autopilot disconnect
